```{r}
  # Ensure WaterYear is a factor in both datasets
  train_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(train_data$WaterYear)
  test_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(test_data$WaterYear)
  
  # Define the formula
  lmForm <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", "(", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "), ")^2"))
  
  # Fit the model using lm()
  model <- lm(lmForm, data = train_data)
  
  # Make predictions
  predictions <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
  
  # Calculate RMSE and R-squared
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((predictions - test_data[[response]])^2))
  r_squared <- summary(model)$r.squared

  list(model = model, rmse = rmse, r_squared = r_squared, predictors = predictors, response = response)
}

# Step 6: Prepare data with log-transformed responses
metDat_model <- dailyDat |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars))) |> 
  drop_na() |>
  # Apply log transformation to the response variables
  mutate(across(all_of(response_vars), ~ log(. + 0.01), .names = "log_{col}")) |>
  mutate(WaterYear = as.factor(WaterYear)) |> 
  mutate(across(all_of(predictor_vars[predictor_vars != "WaterYear"]), ~ scale(.)[, 1]))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

# Update response variables to use log-transformed versions
log_response_vars <- paste0("log_", response_vars)

# Step 7: Run models for each log-transformed response and predictor combination
results <- lapply(log_response_vars, function(response) {
  lapply(predictor_combos, function(predictors) {
    run_model(response, predictors, train_data, test_data)
  })
})


results <- extract_results(results)


# Step 10: Rank models and analyze
bestLogLin <- results |>
  group_by(response) |>
  arrange(response, rmse, r_squared) |> 
  slice_head(n = 5)
```

## Linear-log

```{r}
# Step 1: Define response and predictor variables
response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOpctsat.amp", "probHypSat", "PC1", "PC2", "cluster")
predictor_vars <- c(
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d", "WaterYear"
)


# Update predictor variables to use log-transformed versions
log_predictor_vars <- paste0("log_", predictor_vars[predictor_vars != "WaterYear"])

# Update grouped predictors to use log-transformed variable names
grouped_predictors <- list(
  perc_wet = paste0("log_", c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d")),
  PPT = paste0("log_", c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d")),
  Q = paste0("log_", c("Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d")),
  excProb = paste0("log_", c("excProb")),
  Depth = paste0("log_", c("Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d"))
)


# Step 3 (modified): Generate combinations efficiently with log-transformed predictors
generate_filtered_combos <- function() {
  all_combos <- map(2:5, ~combn(log_predictor_vars, .x, simplify = FALSE)) |>
    purrr::flatten()
  
  filtered_combos <- purrr::keep(all_combos, function(combo) {
    # Check if no more than 1 variable is selected from each group
    group_counts <- sapply(grouped_predictors, function(group) sum(combo %in% group))
    valid_groups <- all(group_counts <= 1)
    
    valid_groups  # Allow only valid combinations
  })
  
  return(filtered_combos)
}


# Step 5: Create functions to run models with and without interaction terms
run_model <- function(response, predictors, train_data, test_data) {
  # Ensure WaterYear is a factor in both datasets
  train_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(train_data$WaterYear)
  test_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(test_data$WaterYear)
  
  # Define the formula
  lmForm <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", "(", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "), ")^2"))
  
  # Fit the model using lm()
  model <- lm(lmForm, data = train_data)
  
  # Make predictions
  predictions <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
  
  # Calculate RMSE and R-squared
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((predictions - test_data[[response]])^2))
  r_squared <- summary(model)$r.squared

  list(model = model, rmse = rmse, r_squared = r_squared, predictors = predictors, response = response)
}

# Step 6: Prepare data with log-transformed predictors
metDat_model <- dailyDat |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars))) |> 
  drop_na() |>
  # Apply log transformation to numeric predictor variables (excluding WaterYear)
  mutate(across(all_of(predictor_vars[predictor_vars != "WaterYear"]), ~ log(. + 0.01), .names = "log_{col}")) |>
  mutate(WaterYear = as.factor(WaterYear))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

# Step 8: Run models for each response and predictor combination
results <- lapply(response_vars, function(response) {
  lapply(predictor_combos, function(predictors) {
    run_model(response, predictors, train_data, test_data)
  })
})

# Step 9: Extract results into dataframes
extract_results <- function(results_list) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(results_list, function(res) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(res, function(model_result) {
      data.frame(
        response = model_result$response,
        predictors = paste(model_result$predictors, collapse = ", "),
        rmse = model_result$rmse,
        r_squared = model_result$r_squared
      )
    }))
  }))
}

results <- extract_results(results)

# Step 10: Rank models and analyze
bestLinLog <- results |>
  group_by(response) |>
  arrange(response, rmse, r_squared) |> 
  slice_head(n = 5)
```

## Log-Log

```{r}
# Step 1: Define response and predictor variables
response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOpctsat.amp", "probHypSat", "PC1", "PC2", "cluster")

predictor_vars <- c(
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d", "WaterYear"
)


# Update predictor variables to use log-transformed versions
log_predictor_vars <- paste0("log_", predictor_vars[predictor_vars != "WaterYear"])

# Update grouped predictors to use log-transformed variable names
grouped_predictors <- list(
  perc_wet = paste0("log_", c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d")),
  PPT = paste0("log_", c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d")),
  Q = paste0("log_", c("Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d")),
  excProb = paste0("log_", c("excProb")),
  Depth = paste0("log_", c("Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d"))
)

# Step 3 (modified): Generate combinations efficiently with log-transformed predictors
generate_filtered_combos <- function() {
  all_combos <- map(2:5, ~combn(log_predictor_vars, .x, simplify = FALSE)) |>
    purrr::flatten()
  
  filtered_combos <- purrr::keep(all_combos, function(combo) {
    # Check if no more than 1 variable is selected from each group
    group_counts <- sapply(grouped_predictors, function(group) sum(combo %in% group))
    valid_groups <- all(group_counts <= 1)
    
  
    
    valid_groups   # Allow only valid combinations
  })
  
  return(filtered_combos)
}

# Step 4: Generate valid predictor combinations
predictor_combos <- generate_filtered_combos()


# Step 5: Create functions to run models with and without interaction terms
run_model <- function(response, predictors, train_data, test_data) {
  # Ensure WaterYear is a factor in both datasets
  train_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(train_data$WaterYear)
  test_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(test_data$WaterYear)
  
  # Define the formula
  lmForm <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", "(", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "), ")^2"))
  
  # Fit the model using lm()
  model <- lm(lmForm, data = train_data)
  
  # Make predictions
  predictions <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
  
  # Calculate RMSE and R-squared
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((predictions - test_data[[response]])^2))
  r_squared <- summary(model)$r.squared

  list(model = model, rmse = rmse, r_squared = r_squared, predictors = predictors, response = response)
}

# Step 6: Prepare data with log-transformed responses and predictors
metDat_model <- dailyDat |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars))) |> 
  drop_na() |>
  # Apply log transformation to numeric variables (responses and predictors)
  mutate(across(all_of(response_vars), ~ log(. + 0.01), .names = "log_{col}")) |>
  mutate(across(all_of(predictor_vars[predictor_vars != "WaterYear"]), ~ log(. + 0.01), .names = "log_{col}")) |>
  mutate(WaterYear = as.factor(WaterYear))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

# Step 8: Run models for each log-transformed response and predictor combination
results <- lapply(log_response_vars, function(response) {
  lapply(predictor_combos, function(predictors) {
    run_model(response, predictors, train_data, test_data)
  })
})

# Step 9: Extract results into dataframes
extract_results <- function(results_list) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(results_list, function(res) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(res, function(model_result) {
      data.frame(
        response = model_result$response,
        predictors = paste(model_result$predictors, collapse = ", "),
        rmse = model_result$rmse,
        r_squared = model_result$r_squared
      )
    }))
  }))
}

results <- extract_results(results)

# Step 10: Rank models and analyze
bestLogLog <- results |>
  group_by(response) |>
  arrange(response, rmse, r_squared) |> 
  slice_head(n = 5)
```

## LM w/ PC Scores

```{r}
# Step 1: Define response and predictor variables
response_vars <- c("PC1", "PC2")
predictor_vars <- c(
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d", "WaterYear"
)

# Step 2: Correct and define the grouping structure
grouped_predictors <- list(
  perc_wet = c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d"),
  PPT = c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d"),
  Q = c("Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d"),
  excProb = c("excProb"),
  #quantQ = c("quantQ", "quantileQ_7d", "quantileQ_14d", "quantileQ_30d"),
  Depth = c("Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d")
)

# Step 3: Generate combinations efficiently
generate_filtered_combos <- function() {
  all_combos <- map(2:4, ~combn(predictor_vars, .x, simplify = FALSE)) |>
    purrr::flatten()
  
  filtered_combos <- purrr::keep(all_combos, function(combo) {
    # Check if no more than 1 variable is selected from each group
    group_counts <- sapply(grouped_predictors, function(group) sum(combo %in% group))
    valid_groups <- all(group_counts <= 1)
    valid_groups   # Allow only valid combinations
  })

  return(filtered_combos)
}

# Step 4: Generate the valid predictor combinations
predictor_combos <- generate_filtered_combos()

# Step 5: Create functions to run models
run_model <- function(response, predictors, train_data, test_data) {
  # Ensure WaterYear is a factor in both datasets
  train_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(train_data$WaterYear)
  test_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(test_data$WaterYear)
  
  # Define the formula
  lmForm <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", "(", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "), ")^2"))
  
  # Fit the model using lm()
  model <- lm(lmForm, data = train_data)
  
  # Make predictions
  predictions <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
  
  # Calculate RMSE and R-squared
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((predictions - test_data[[response]])^2))
  r_squared <- summary(model)$r.squared

  list(model = model, rmse = rmse, r_squared = r_squared, predictors = predictors, response = response)
}

# Step 6: Prepare data
metDat_model <- dailyDat |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars))) |> 
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(WaterYear = as.factor(WaterYear)) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ scale(.)[, 1]))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

# Step 7: Run models for each response and predictor combination
results <- lapply(response_vars, function(response) {
  lapply(predictor_combos, function(predictors) {
    run_model(response, predictors, train_data, test_data)
  })
})


# Step 8: Extract results into dataframes
extract_results <- function(results_list) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(results_list, function(res) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(res, function(model_result) {
      data.frame(
        response = model_result$response,
        predictors = paste(model_result$predictors, collapse = ", "),
        rmse = model_result$rmse,
        r_squared = model_result$r_squared
      )
    }))
  }))
}

results <- extract_results(results) 


# Step 10: Rank models and analyze
bestLM_PC <- results |>
  group_by(response) |>
  arrange(response, rmse, r_squared) |> 
  slice_head(n = 5)
```

```{r}
# Step 1: Identify the best model for each response variable
best_models <- results_wInteraction |>
  full_join(results_woInteraction) |> 
  group_by(response) |>
  arrange(rmse, r_squared) |>
  slice_head(n = 1)

# Step 2: Extract the predictors for the best models and plot observed vs. predicted
best_model_plots <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(best_models)), function(i) {
  best_model <- best_models[i, ]
  response <- best_model$response
  predictors <- unlist(strsplit(best_model$predictors, ", "))
  
  # Generate the formula for the best model
  formula_str <- paste(response, "~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "))
  model_formula <- as.formula(formula_str)
  
  # Fit the best model
  best_model_fit <- lm(model_formula, data = train_data)
  
  # Get predictions for visualization
  train_data$predicted <- predict(best_model_fit, newdata = train_data)
  
  # Annotate the model formula on the plot
  formula_text <- paste("Model:", formula_str)
  
  # Plot observed vs. predicted for the best model
  plot <- ggplot(train_data, aes_string(x = response, y = "predicted")) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    annotate("text", x = max(train_data[[response]], na.rm = TRUE), 
             y = max(train_data$predicted, na.rm = TRUE), 
             label = formula_text, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, size = 3.5, color = "blue") +
    labs(
      title = paste("Observed vs Predicted:", response),
      x = "Observed",
      y = "Predicted"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
  
  return(plot)
})

# Step 3: Display the plots
for (plot in best_model_plots) {
  print(plot)
}


# Step 4: Plot individual predictors for each response
individual_predictor_plots <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(best_models)), function(i) {
  best_model <- best_models[i, ]
  response <- best_model$response
  predictors <- unlist(strsplit(best_model$predictors, ", "))
  
  # Generate individual plots for each predictor
  predictor_plots <- lapply(predictors, function(predictor) {
    ggplot(train_data, aes_string(x = predictor, y = response)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "blue") +
      stat_regline_equation(aes(label = ..rr.label..))+
      labs(
        title = paste("Response vs Predictor:", response, "vs", predictor),
        x = predictor,
        y = response
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
  })
  
  return(predictor_plots)
})

# Step 5: Display individual predictor plots
for (predictor_set in individual_predictor_plots) {
  for (plot in predictor_set) {
    print(plot)
  }
}
```

## Linear-Log w/ PC Scores

```{r}
# Step 1: Define response and predictor variables
response_vars <- c("PC1", "PC2")
predictor_vars <- c(
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d", "WaterYear"
)
# Update predictor variables to use log-transformed versions
log_predictor_vars <- paste0("log_", predictor_vars[predictor_vars != "WaterYear"])

# Update grouped predictors to use log-transformed variable names
grouped_predictors <- list(
  perc_wet = paste0("log_", c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d")),
  PPT = paste0("log_", c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d")),
  Q = paste0("log_", c("Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d")),
  excProb = c("excProb"),
  Depth = paste0("log_", c("Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d"))
)

# Step 3 (modified): Generate combinations efficiently with log-transformed predictors
generate_filtered_combos <- function() {
  all_combos <- map(2:4, ~combn(log_predictor_vars, .x, simplify = FALSE)) |>
    purrr::flatten()
  
  filtered_combos <- purrr::keep(all_combos, function(combo) {
    # Check if no more than 1 variable is selected from each group
    group_counts <- sapply(grouped_predictors, function(group) sum(combo %in% group))
    valid_groups <- all(group_counts <= 1)
    
    # Check for invalid combinations: 
    # If both "Q" and "Depth" are present, or both "Depth" and "Q" are present, the combo is invalid
    no_invalid_combinations <- !(
        any(combo %in% c("WaterYear"))
    )
    
    valid_groups && no_invalid_combinations  # Allow only valid combinations
  })
  
  return(filtered_combos)
}

# Step 4: Generate the valid predictor combinations
predictor_combos <- generate_filtered_combos()

# Step 5: Create functions to run models with and without interaction terms
run_model <- function(response, predictors, train_data, test_data, interaction = FALSE) {
  # Ensure WaterYear is a factor in both datasets
  train_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(train_data$WaterYear)
  test_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(test_data$WaterYear)
  
  # Define the formula
  interaction_term <- if (interaction) " * WaterYear" else ""
  lmForm <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "), interaction_term))
  
  # Fit the model using lm()
  model <- lm(lmForm, data = train_data)
  
  # Make predictions
  predictions <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
  
  # Calculate RMSE and R-squared
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((predictions - test_data[[response]])^2))
  r_squared <- summary(model)$r.squared

  list(model = model, rmse = rmse, r_squared = r_squared, predictors = predictors, response = response, interaction_term = interaction_term)
}

# Step 6: Prepare data with log-transformed predictors
metDat_model <- dailyDat |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars))) |> 
  drop_na() |>
  # Apply log transformation to numeric predictor variables (excluding WaterYear)
  mutate(across(all_of(predictor_vars[predictor_vars != "WaterYear"]), ~ log(. + 0.01), .names = "log_{col}")) |>
  mutate(WaterYear = as.factor(WaterYear))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

# Step 8: Run models for each response and predictor combination
results_with_interaction <- lapply(response_vars, function(response) {
  lapply(predictor_combos, function(predictors) {
    run_model(response, predictors, train_data, test_data, interaction = TRUE)
  })
})

results_without_interaction <- lapply(response_vars, function(response) {
  lapply(predictor_combos, function(predictors) {
    run_model(response, predictors, train_data, test_data, interaction = FALSE)
  })
})

# Step 9: Extract results into dataframes
extract_results <- function(results_list) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(results_list, function(res) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(res, function(model_result) {
      data.frame(
        response = model_result$response,
        predictors = paste(model_result$predictors, collapse = ", "),
        rmse = model_result$rmse,
        r_squared = model_result$r_squared,
        interaction_term = model_result$interaction_term
      )
    }))
  }))
}

results_wInteraction <- extract_results(results_with_interaction)
results_woInteraction <- extract_results(results_without_interaction)

# Step 10: Rank models and analyze
bestLinLogPC <- results_wInteraction |>
  full_join(results_woInteraction) |> 
  group_by(response) |>
  arrange(response, rmse, r_squared) |> 
  slice_head(n = 5)
```

```{r}
# Step 1: Identify the best model for each response variable
best_models <- results_wInteraction |>
  full_join(results_woInteraction) |> 
  group_by(response) |>
  arrange(rmse, r_squared) |>
  slice_head(n = 1)

# Step 2: Extract the predictors for the best models and plot observed vs. predicted
best_model_plots <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(best_models)), function(i) {
  best_model <- best_models[i, ]
  response <- best_model$response
  predictors <- unlist(strsplit(best_model$predictors, ", "))
  
  # Generate the formula for the best model
  formula_str <- paste(response, "~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "))
  model_formula <- as.formula(formula_str)
  
  # Fit the best model
  best_model_fit <- lm(model_formula, data = train_data)
  
  # Get predictions for visualization
  train_data$predicted <- predict(best_model_fit, newdata = train_data)
  
  # Annotate the model formula on the plot
  formula_text <- paste("Model:", formula_str)
  
  # Plot observed vs. predicted for the best model
  plot <- ggplot(train_data, aes_string(x = response, y = "predicted")) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    annotate("text", x = max(train_data[[response]], na.rm = TRUE), 
             y = max(train_data$predicted, na.rm = TRUE), 
             label = formula_text, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, size = 3.5, color = "blue") +
    labs(
      title = paste("Observed vs Predicted:", response),
      x = "Observed",
      y = "Predicted"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
  
  return(plot)
})

# Step 3: Display the plots
for (plot in best_model_plots) {
  print(plot)
}


# Step 4: Plot individual predictors for each response
individual_predictor_plots <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(best_models)), function(i) {
  best_model <- best_models[i, ]
  response <- best_model$response
  predictors <- unlist(strsplit(best_model$predictors, ", "))
  
  # Generate individual plots for each predictor
  predictor_plots <- lapply(predictors, function(predictor) {
    ggplot(train_data, aes_string(x = predictor, y = response)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "blue") +
      stat_regline_equation(aes(label = ..rr.label..))+
      labs(
        title = paste("Response vs Predictor:", response, "vs", predictor),
        x = predictor,
        y = response
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
  })
  
  return(predictor_plots)
})

# Step 5: Display individual predictor plots
for (predictor_set in individual_predictor_plots) {
  for (plot in predictor_set) {
    print(plot)
  }
}
```

## Cluster LM

```{r}
# Step 1: Define response and predictor variables
response_vars <- c("cluster")
predictor_vars <- c(
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d", "WaterYear"
)

# Step 2: Correct and define the grouping structure
grouped_predictors <- list(
  perc_wet = c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d"),
  PPT = c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d"),
  Q = c("Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d"),
  excProb = c("excProb"),
  #quantQ = c("quantQ", "quantileQ_7d", "quantileQ_14d", "quantileQ_30d"),
  Depth = c("Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d")
)

# Step 3: Generate combinations efficiently
generate_filtered_combos <- function() {
  all_combos <- map(1:5, ~combn(predictor_vars, .x, simplify = FALSE)) |>
    purrr::flatten()
  
  filtered_combos <- purrr::keep(all_combos, function(combo) {
    # Check if no more than 1 variable is selected from each group
    group_counts <- sapply(grouped_predictors, function(group) sum(combo %in% group))
    valid_groups <- all(group_counts <= 1)
    
    # Check for invalid combinations: 
    # If both "Q" and "quantDepth" are present, or both "Depth" and "quantQ" are present, the combo is invalid
    no_invalid_combinations <- !(
    
        any(combo %in% c("WaterYear"))
    )
    
    valid_groups && no_invalid_combinations  # Allow only valid combinations
  })
  
  return(filtered_combos)
}

# Step 4: Generate the valid predictor combinations
predictor_combos <- generate_filtered_combos()

# Step 5: Create functions to run models with and without interaction terms
run_model <- function(response, predictors, train_data, test_data, interaction = FALSE) {
  # Ensure WaterYear is a factor in both datasets
  train_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(train_data$WaterYear)
  test_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(test_data$WaterYear)
  
  # Define the formula
  interaction_term <- if (interaction) " * WaterYear" else ""
  lmForm <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "), interaction_term))
  
  # Fit the model using lm()
  model <- lm(lmForm, data = train_data)
  
  # Make predictions
  predictions <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
  
  # Calculate RMSE and R-squared
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((predictions - test_data[[response]])^2))
  r_squared <- summary(model)$r.squared

  list(model = model, rmse = rmse, r_squared = r_squared, predictors = predictors, response = response, interaction_term = interaction_term)
}

# Step 6: Prepare data
metDat_model <- clustered_data |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars))) |> 
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(WaterYear = as.factor(WaterYear)) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ scale(.)[, 1]))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

# Step 7: Run models for each response and predictor combination
results_with_interaction <- lapply(response_vars, function(response) {
  lapply(predictor_combos, function(predictors) {
    run_model(response, predictors, train_data, test_data, interaction = TRUE)
  })
})

results_without_interaction <- lapply(response_vars, function(response) {
  lapply(predictor_combos, function(predictors) {
    run_model(response, predictors, train_data, test_data, interaction = FALSE)
  })
})

# Step 8: Extract results into dataframes
extract_results <- function(results_list) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(results_list, function(res) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(res, function(model_result) {
      data.frame(
        response = model_result$response,
        predictors = paste(model_result$predictors, collapse = ", "),
        rmse = model_result$rmse,
        r_squared = model_result$r_squared,
        interaction_term = model_result$interaction_term
      )
    }))
  }))
}

results_wInteraction <- extract_results(results_with_interaction) 
results_woInteraction <- extract_results(results_without_interaction)


# Step 10: Rank models and analyze
bestLM <- results_wInteraction |>
  full_join(results_woInteraction) |> 
  group_by(response) |>
  arrange(response, rmse, r_squared) |> 
  slice_head(n = 1)
```

```{r}
metDat_model <- clustered_data |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars))) |> 
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(WaterYear = as.factor(WaterYear)) |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ scale(.)[, 1]))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

  # Fit the best model
best_model_fit <- lm(cluster~(PercWet_1d+PPT_60d+excProb+Depth_60d+WaterYear)^2, data = train_data)
  
  # Get predictions for visualization
  test_data$predicted <- predict(best_model_fit, newdata = test_data)
  
  # Plot observed vs. predicted for the best model
ggplot(test_data, aes(x = cluster, y = "predicted")) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    labs(
      x = "Observed",
      y = "Predicted"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
  
  return(plot)
```

# MRLM

```{r}
library(glmnet)

response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOpctsat.amp", "probHypSat")
predictor_vars <- c(
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d", "WaterYear"
)

grouped_predictors <- list(
  perc_wet = c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d"),
  PPT = c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d"),
  Q = c("Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d"),
  excProb = c("excProb"),
  #quantQ = c("quantQ", "quantileQ_7d", "quantileQ_14d", "quantileQ_30d"),
  Depth = c("Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d")
)

# Filter the dataset to include only the selected predictors and response variables
  modelDat <- dailyDat |>
    select(all_of(c(predictor_vars, response_vars))) |>
    drop_na()  # Remove rows with missing values
  
set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(modelDat, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)


# Generate all combinations of predictors
predictor_combinations <- expand.grid(
  perc_wet = grouped_predictors$perc_wet,
  PPT = grouped_predictors$PPT,
  Q = grouped_predictors$Q,
  excProb = grouped_predictors$excProb,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Prepare results storage
# Initialize a data frame to store model performance
# Initialize a data frame to store model performance
# Initialize a data frame to store model performance
model_performance <- data.frame(
  Model_ID = character(),
  Predictors = character(),
  Mean_CV_Error = numeric(),
  R2 = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Loop through each combination of predictors
for (i in 1:nrow(predictor_combinations)) {
  # Get current predictors
  current_predictors <- predictor_combinations[i, ]
  predictors <- c(
    current_predictors$perc_wet,
    current_predictors$PPT,
    current_predictors$Q,
    "WaterYear"
  )
  
  # Filter the dataset to include only the selected predictors and response variables
  data_subset <- dailyDatRoll |>
    select(all_of(c(predictors, response_vars))) |>
    drop_na()  # Remove rows with missing values
  
  # Prepare the design matrix (X) and response matrix (Y)
  X <- model.matrix(~ . * WaterYear, data = data_subset[, predictors])[, -1]
  Y <- as.matrix(data_subset[, response_vars])
  
  # Fit glmnet model with cross-validation
  cv_model <- cv.glmnet(X, Y, family = "mgaussian", alpha = 1, nfolds = 10)  # 10-fold CV
  
  # Extract mean cross-validation error
  mean_cv_error <- mean(cv_model$cvm)
  
  # Compute R² for the model
  predictions_list <- predict(cv_model, newx = X, s = "lambda.min")  # Predictions for each response
  residuals <- 0
  ss_total <- 0
  
  # Calculate residuals and total sum of squares for each response variable
  for (j in 1:length(response_vars)) {
    pred <- predictions_list[[j]]  # Predictions for response j
    actual <- Y[, j]               # Actual values for response j
    residuals <- residuals + sum((actual - pred)^2)
    ss_total <- ss_total + sum((actual - mean(actual))^2)
  }
  
  # R² calculation
  r2 <- 1 - (residuals / ss_total)
  
  # Save model performance
  model_performance <- rbind(
    model_performance,
    data.frame(
      Model_ID = paste0("Model_", i),
      Predictors = paste(current_predictors, collapse = ", "),
      Mean_CV_Error = mean_cv_error,
      R2 = r2
    )
  )
  
  # Store the model, predictors, and R² in results
  results[[paste0("Model_", i)]] <- list(
    predictors = current_predictors,
    model = cv_model,
    mean_cv_error = mean_cv_error,
    r2 = r2
  )
}

# Sort results by Mean_CV_Error and R² to find the best models
best_models <- model_performance |>
  arrange(Mean_CV_Error, desc(R2)) |>
  head(5)  # Adjust to show the top N models

# View the best models
print(best_models)

```

```{r}
# Step 1: Define response and predictor variables
response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOSat.hyp", "DOpctsat.amp")
predictor_vars <- c(
  "percent_wet", "perc_wet7d", "perc_wet14d", "perc_wet30d", "perc_wet60d", "perc_wet_change",
  "AvgPPT_7d", "AvgPPT_14d", "AvgPPT_30d", "totPPT_7d", 
  "totPPT_14d", "totPPT_30d", "meanQ", "meanQ_7d", "meanQ_14d", "meanQ_30d",
  "meanQ_ecdf", "WaterYear"
)

# Step 2: Prepare data
metDat_model <- dailyDatRoll |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars))) |> 
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(WaterYear = as.factor(WaterYear)) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ scale(.)[, 1]))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

# Prepare matrices for glmnet
x_train <- as.matrix(train_data[, predictor_vars])
x_test <- as.matrix(test_data[, predictor_vars])
y_train <- as.matrix(train_data[, response_vars])
y_test <- as.matrix(test_data[, response_vars])

# Step 3: Fit a multi-response linear model with glmnet
fit_glmnet <- function(x_train, y_train, alpha = 1) {
  # alpha = 1 (Lasso), alpha = 0 (Ridge), 0 < alpha < 1 (Elastic Net)
  glmnet::glmnet(
    x = x_train,
    y = y_train,
    family = "mgaussian", # Multivariate Gaussian family for multi-response
    alpha = alpha
  )
}

# Fit the model
glmnet_model <- fit_glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 0.5)  # Elastic Net

# Step 4: Cross-validation to tune lambda
cv_glmnet <- function(x_train, y_train, alpha = 1) {
  glmnet::cv.glmnet(
    x = x_train,
    y = y_train,
    family = "mgaussian",
    alpha = alpha,
    nfolds = 5
  )
}

cv_model <- cv_glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 0.5)

# Optimal lambda
lambda_opt <- cv_model$lambda.min

# Step 5: Make predictions on test data
predictions <- predict(glmnet_model, s = lambda_opt, newx = x_test)

# Convert predictions to a dataframe
predictions_df <- as.data.frame(predictions)

# Step 6: Evaluate the model
evaluate_model <- function(y_test, predictions) {
  # `predictions` is a 3D array: rows (observations) x cols (responses) x s (lambda values)
  # Select predictions for the chosen lambda (typically the first slice if not specified)
  predictions_matrix <- predictions[, , 1] # Use the first lambda slice
  
  metrics <- lapply(seq_len(ncol(y_test)), function(i) {
    actual <- y_test[, i]
    predicted <- predictions_matrix[, i]
    rmse <- sqrt(mean((actual - predicted)^2))
    ss_total <- sum((actual - mean(actual))^2)
    ss_res <- sum((actual - predicted)^2)
    r_squared <- 1 - (ss_res / ss_total)
    data.frame(response = colnames(y_test)[i], rmse = rmse, r_squared = r_squared)
  })
  do.call(rbind, metrics)
}

# Updated to work with glmnet predictions
model_metrics <- evaluate_model(y_test, predictions)

# Step 7: Analyze and visualize results
print(model_metrics)

# Visualize RMSE and R-squared
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(model_metrics, aes(x = response, y = rmse)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "RMSE for Each Response", x = "Response Variable", y = "RMSE") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(model_metrics, aes(x = response, y = r_squared)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "forestgreen") +
  labs(title = "R-squared for Each Response", x = "Response Variable", y = "R-squared") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(glmnet)
library(rsample)
library(ggplot2)

# Step 1: Define response and predictor variables
response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOSat.hyp", "DOpctsat.amp")
predictor_vars <- c(
  "percent_wet", "perc_wet7d", "perc_wet14d", "perc_wet30d", "perc_wet60d", "perc_wet_change",
  "AvgPPT_7d", "AvgPPT_14d", "AvgPPT_30d", "totPPT_7d", "totPPT_14d", "totPPT_30d",
  "meanQ", "meanQ_7d", "meanQ_14d", "meanQ_30d",
  "meanQ_ecdf", "WaterYear"
)

# Step 2: Prepare data
metDat_model <- dailyDatRoll |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars))) |> 
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(WaterYear = as.factor(WaterYear)) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ scale(.)[, 1]))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

# Helper function to fit and evaluate model
fit_and_evaluate <- function(predictor_subset) {
  # Prepare matrices for glmnet
  x_train <- as.matrix(train_data[, predictor_subset])
  x_test <- as.matrix(test_data[, predictor_subset])
  y_train <- as.matrix(train_data[, response_vars])
  y_test <- as.matrix(test_data[, response_vars])
  
  # Fit the model
  cv_model <- glmnet::cv.glmnet(
    x = x_train,
    y = y_train,
    family = "mgaussian",
    alpha = 0.5,  # Elastic Net
    nfolds = 5
  )
  
  # Optimal lambda
  lambda_opt <- cv_model$lambda.min
  
  # Make predictions
  predictions <- predict(cv_model, s = lambda_opt, newx = x_test)
  
  # Evaluate the model
  predictions_matrix <- predictions[, , 1]  # Use the first lambda slice
  metrics <- lapply(seq_len(ncol(y_test)), function(i) {
    actual <- y_test[, i]
    predicted <- predictions_matrix[, i]
    rmse <- sqrt(mean((actual - predicted)^2))
    ss_total <- sum((actual - mean(actual))^2)
    ss_res <- sum((actual - predicted)^2)
    r_squared <- 1 - (ss_res / ss_total)
    data.frame(response = colnames(y_test)[i], rmse = rmse, r_squared = r_squared)
  })
  metrics_df <- do.call(rbind, metrics)
  metrics_df$predictors <- paste(predictor_subset, collapse = ", ")
  return(metrics_df)
}

# Step 3: Iterate over all combinations of predictor variables (with 2 or more predictors)
predictor_combinations <- unlist(lapply(2:length(predictor_vars), function(k) {
  combn(predictor_vars, k, simplify = FALSE)
}), recursive = FALSE)

# Fit and evaluate for each combination
results <- lapply(predictor_combinations, fit_and_evaluate)

# Combine results
all_metrics <- do.call(rbind, results)

# Step 4: Analyze and visualize results
library(ace_tools)
ace_tools.display_dataframe_to_user("All Model Metrics", all_metrics)

# Visualize top results by RMSE
top_models <- all_metrics |> arrange(rmse) |> head(10)

ggplot(top_models, aes(x = reorder(predictors, -rmse), y = rmse)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Models by RMSE", x = "Predictor Combination", y = "RMSE") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), panel.background = element_blank())

# Visualize top results by R-squared
ggplot(top_models, aes(x = reorder(predictors, -r_squared), y = r_squared)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "forestgreen") +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Models by R-squared", x = "Predictor Combination", y = "R-squared") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), panel.background = element_blank())

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(glmnet)
library(rsample)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)

response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOSat.hyp", "DOpctsat.amp")

# Group predictors by type
percent_wet_vars <- c("percent_wet", "perc_wet7d", "perc_wet14d", "perc_wet30d", "perc_wet60d", "perc_wet_change")
ppt_vars <- c("AvgPPT_7d", "AvgPPT_14d", "AvgPPT_30d", "totPPT_7d", "totPPT_14d", "totPPT_30d")
q_vars <- c("meanQ", "meanQ_7d", "meanQ_14d", "meanQ_30d", "meanQ_ecdf")
fixed_vars <- c("WaterYear")  # Fixed variables included in all combinations

# For each group, create a list of options: NULL (no selection), or each variable
percent_wet_options <- c(list(NULL), as.list(percent_wet_vars))
ppt_options <- c(list(NULL), as.list(ppt_vars))
q_options <- c(list(NULL), as.list(q_vars))

# Generate all combinations of zero or one variable from each group
combos_df <- expand.grid(
  PercentWet = percent_wet_options,
  PPT = ppt_options,
  Q = q_options,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Collect selected predictors for each combination
combos_df <- combos_df |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(
    # Combine selected predictors, excluding NULLs
    Predictors = list(purrr::compact(c(PercentWet, PPT, Q))),
    # Count the number of predictors selected (excluding fixed variables)
    NumPredictors = length(Predictors)
  ) |>
  ungroup()

# Filter combinations to have 2 to 4 predictors (excluding fixed variables)
combos_df <- combos_df |>
  filter(NumPredictors >= 1, NumPredictors <= 4)

# Add fixed variables to each combination
combos_df <- combos_df |>
  mutate(
    Predictors = map(Predictors, ~ c(.x, fixed_vars))
  )

# Remove duplicate combinations
# Sort predictors to ensure duplicates can be identified
combos_df <- combos_df |>
  mutate(
    PredictorsSorted = map(Predictors, sort),
    PredictorsStr = map_chr(PredictorsSorted, paste, collapse = ",")
  ) |>
  distinct(PredictorsStr, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Extract the list of unique predictor combinations
predictor_combinations <- combos_df$Predictors

# Optionally, remove temporary columns if not needed
combos_df <- combos_df |> select(-PredictorsSorted, -PredictorsStr)

# Step 2: Prepare data
metDat_model <- dailyDatRoll |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, percent_wet_vars, ppt_vars, q_vars, fixed_vars))) |> 
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(WaterYear = factor(WaterYear, levels = unique(WaterYear))) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ scale(.)[, 1]))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

# Helper function to fit and evaluate model
fit_and_evaluate <- function(predictor_subset) {
  # Prepare matrices for glmnet using model.matrix
  x_train <- model.matrix(~ . -1, data = train_data[, predictor_subset])
  x_test <- model.matrix(~ . -1, data = test_data[, predictor_subset])
  y_train <- as.matrix(train_data[, response_vars])
  y_test <- as.matrix(test_data[, response_vars])
  
  # Fit the model
  cv_model <- glmnet::cv.glmnet(
    x = x_train,
    y = y_train,
    family = "mgaussian",
    alpha = 0.5,  # Elastic Net
    nfolds = 5
  )
  
  # Optimal lambda
  lambda_opt <- cv_model$lambda.min
  
  # Make predictions
  predictions <- predict(cv_model, s = lambda_opt, newx = x_test)
  
  # Evaluate the model
  predictions_matrix <- predictions[, , 1]  # Use the first lambda slice
  
  metrics <- lapply(seq_len(ncol(y_test)), function(i) {
    actual <- y_test[, i]
    predicted <- predictions_matrix[, i]
    # Check lengths before calculations
    if(length(actual) != length(predicted)) {
      stop("Mismatch in lengths of actual and predicted values.")
    }
    rmse <- sqrt(mean((actual - predicted)^2))
    ss_total <- sum((actual - mean(actual))^2)
    ss_res <- sum((actual - predicted)^2)
    r_squared <- 1 - (ss_res / ss_total)
    data.frame(response = colnames(y_test)[i], rmse = rmse, r_squared = r_squared)
  })
  metrics_df <- do.call(rbind, metrics)
  metrics_df$predictors <- paste(predictor_subset, collapse = ", ")
  return(metrics_df)
}

# Step 4: Fit and evaluate models
results <- lapply(predictor_combinations, fit_and_evaluate)

# Combine results
all_metrics <- do.call(rbind, results)


# Visualize top results by RMSE
top_models <- all_metrics |> arrange(rmse) |> head(10)

ggplot(top_models, aes(x = reorder(predictors, -rmse), y = rmse)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Models by RMSE", x = "Predictor Combination", y = "RMSE") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), panel.background = element_blank())

# Visualize top results by R-squared
ggplot(top_models, aes(x = reorder(predictors, -r_squared), y = r_squared)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "forestgreen") +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Models by R-squared", x = "Predictor Combination", y = "R-squared") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), panel.background = element_blank())

```

## Run This

```{r}
response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOSat.hyp")

# Group predictors by type
percent_wet_vars <- c("percent_wet", "perc_wet7d", "perc_wet14d", "perc_wet30d", "perc_wet60d", "perc_wet_change")
ppt_vars <- c("AvgPPT_7d", "AvgPPT_14d", "AvgPPT_30d", "totPPT_7d", "totPPT_14d", "totPPT_30d")
q_vars <- c("meanQ", "meanQ_7d", "meanQ_14d", "meanQ_30d", "meanQ_ecdf")
wateryear_vars <- c("WaterYear")  # WaterYear now treated as a regular predictor

# For each group, create a list of options: NULL (no selection), or each variable
percent_wet_options <- c(list(NULL), as.list(percent_wet_vars))
ppt_options <- c(list(NULL), as.list(ppt_vars))
q_options <- c(list(NULL), as.list(q_vars))
wateryear_options <- c(list(NULL), as.list(wateryear_vars))

# Generate all combinations of zero or one variable from each group
combos_df <- expand.grid(
  PercentWet = percent_wet_options,
  PPT = ppt_options,
  Q = q_options,
  WaterYear = wateryear_options,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Collect selected predictors for each combination
combos_df <- combos_df |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(
    # Combine selected predictors, excluding NULLs
    Predictors = list(purrr::compact(c(PercentWet, PPT, Q, WaterYear))),
    # Count the number of predictors selected
    NumPredictors = length(Predictors)
  ) |>
  ungroup()

# Filter combinations to have 1 to 4 predictors
combos_df <- combos_df |>
  filter(NumPredictors >= 2, NumPredictors <= 4)

# Remove duplicate combinations
# Sort predictors to ensure duplicates can be identified
combos_df <- combos_df |>
  mutate(
    PredictorsSorted = map(Predictors, sort),
    PredictorsStr = map_chr(PredictorsSorted, paste, collapse = ",")
  ) |>
  distinct(PredictorsStr, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Extract the list of unique predictor combinations
predictor_combinations <- combos_df$Predictors

# Optionally, remove temporary columns if not needed
combos_df <- combos_df |> select(-PredictorsSorted, -PredictorsStr)

# Step 2: Prepare data
all_predictor_vars <- c(percent_wet_vars, ppt_vars, q_vars, wateryear_vars)

metDat_model <- dailyDatRoll |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, all_predictor_vars))) |> 
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(WaterYear = factor(WaterYear, levels = unique(WaterYear)))# |>
 # mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ scale(.)[, 1]))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

# Helper function to fit and evaluate model
fit_and_evaluate <- function(predictor_subset) {
  # Prepare matrices for glmnet using model.matrix
  x_train <- model.matrix(~ . -1, data = train_data[, predictor_subset])
  x_test <- model.matrix(~ . -1, data = test_data[, predictor_subset])
  y_train <- as.matrix(train_data[, response_vars])
  y_test <- as.matrix(test_data[, response_vars])
  alphas <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)
  
  # Initialize objects to store results
cv_errors <- numeric(length(alphas))
cv_models <- list()

# Step 3: Cross-validation for each alpha
for (i in seq_along(alphas)) {
  set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
  cv_model <- cv.glmnet(
    x = x_train,
    y = y_train,
    family = "mgaussian",  # Use "mgaussian" if y_train is multivariate
    nfolds = 10,
    alpha = alphas[i]
  )
  cv_models[[i]] <- cv_model
  cv_errors[i] <- min(cv_model$cvm)
}



# Identify the alpha with the lowest cross-validated error
best_alpha_index <- which.min(cv_errors)
alpha_opt <- alphas[best_alpha_index]
best_cv_model <- cv_models[[best_alpha_index]]

# Optimal lambda
lambda_opt <- best_cv_model$lambda.min
  
  # Refit the model with the best lambda
  final_model <- glmnet::glmnet(
    x = x_train,
    y = y_train,
    family = "mgaussian",
    alpha = alpha_opt,  # Elastic Net
    nfolds = 10,
    lambda = lambda_opt
  )
  # Make predictions
  predictions <- predict(final_model, s = lambda_opt, newx = x_test)
  
  # Evaluate the model
  predictions_matrix <- predictions[, , 1]  # Use the first lambda slice
  
  metrics <- lapply(seq_len(ncol(y_test)), function(i) {
    actual <- y_test[, i]
    predicted <- predictions_matrix[, i]
    # Check lengths before calculations
    if(length(actual) != length(predicted)) {
      stop("Mismatch in lengths of actual and predicted values.")
    }
    rmse <- sqrt(mean((actual - predicted)^2))
    ss_total <- sum((actual - mean(actual))^2)
    ss_res <- sum((actual - predicted)^2)
    r_squared <- 1 - (ss_res / ss_total)
    data.frame(response = colnames(y_test)[i], rmse = rmse, r_squared = r_squared)
  })
  metrics_df <- do.call(rbind, metrics)
  metrics_df$predictors <- paste(predictor_subset, collapse = ", ")
  return(metrics_df)
}

# Step 4: Fit and evaluate models
results <- lapply(predictor_combinations, fit_and_evaluate)

# Combine results
all_metrics <- do.call(rbind, results)

# Visualize top results by RMSE
top_models <- all_metrics |> arrange(rmse) |> head(10)

ggplot(top_models, aes(x = reorder(predictors, -rmse), y = rmse)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Models by RMSE", x = "Predictor Combination", y = "RMSE") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), panel.background = element_blank())

# Visualize top results by R-squared
ggplot(top_models, aes(x = reorder(predictors, -r_squared), y = r_squared)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "forestgreen") +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Models by R-squared", x = "Predictor Combination", y = "R-squared") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), panel.background = element_blank())

```

```{r}
# Step 1: Combine the results into a single data frame
results_df <- do.call(rbind, results)

# Convert 'response' and 'predictors' columns to factors for better plotting
results_df$response <- factor(results_df$response)
results_df$predictors <- factor(results_df$predictors)

# Add a column for the number of predictors used in each model
results_df$num_predictors <- sapply(strsplit(as.character(results_df$predictors), ",\\s*"), length)

results_df <- results_df |> arrange(rmse, r_squared)

ggplot(results_df, aes(x = reorder(predictors, -r_squared), y = r_squared, fill = response)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Model R-squared Values by Predictor Combination",
       x = "Predictor Combination",
       y = "R-squared") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1))

ggplot(results_df, aes(x = rmse, y = r_squared, color = response)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = as.factor(num_predictors)), size = 3) +
  labs(title = "RMSE vs. R-squared Across Models",
       x = "RMSE",
       y = "R-squared",
       shape = "Number of Predictors") +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOSat.hyp")

# Group predictors by type
percent_wet_vars <- c("percent_wet", "perc_wet7d", "perc_wet14d", "perc_wet30d", "perc_wet60d", "perc_wet_change")
ppt_vars <- c("AvgPPT_7d", "AvgPPT_14d", "AvgPPT_30d", "totPPT_7d", "totPPT_14d", "totPPT_30d")
q_vars <- c("meanQ", "meanQ_7d", "meanQ_14d", "meanQ_30d", "meanQ_ecdf")
wateryear_vars <- c("WaterYear")  # WaterYear now treated as a regular predictor

# For each group, create a list of options: NULL (no selection), or each variable
percent_wet_options <- c(list(NULL), as.list(percent_wet_vars))
ppt_options <- c(list(NULL), as.list(ppt_vars))
q_options <- c(list(NULL), as.list(q_vars))
wateryear_options <- c(list(NULL), as.list(wateryear_vars))

# Generate all combinations of zero or one variable from each group
combos_df <- expand.grid(
  PercentWet = percent_wet_options,
  PPT = ppt_options,
  Q = q_options,
  WaterYear = wateryear_options,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Collect selected predictors for each combination
combos_df <- combos_df |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(
    # Combine selected predictors, excluding NULLs
    Predictors = list(purrr::compact(c(PercentWet, PPT, Q, WaterYear))),
    # Count the number of predictors selected
    NumPredictors = length(Predictors)
  ) |>
  ungroup()

# Filter combinations to have 1 to 4 predictors
combos_df <- combos_df |>
  filter(NumPredictors >= 2, NumPredictors <= 4)

# Remove duplicate combinations
# Sort predictors to ensure duplicates can be identified
combos_df <- combos_df |>
  mutate(
    PredictorsSorted = map(Predictors, sort),
    PredictorsStr = map_chr(PredictorsSorted, paste, collapse = ",")
  ) |>
  distinct(PredictorsStr, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Extract the list of unique predictor combinations
predictor_combinations <- combos_df$Predictors

# Optionally, remove temporary columns if not needed
combos_df <- combos_df |> select(-PredictorsSorted, -PredictorsStr)

# Step 2: Prepare data
all_predictor_vars <- c(percent_wet_vars, ppt_vars, q_vars, wateryear_vars)

metDat_model <- dailyDatRoll |>
  ungroup() |> 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, all_predictor_vars))) |> 
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(WaterYear = factor(WaterYear, levels = unique(WaterYear))) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ scale(.)[, 1]))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

# Helper function to fit and evaluate model
fit_and_evaluate <- function(predictor_subset) {
  # Prepare matrices for glmnet using model.matrix
  x_train <- model.matrix(~ . -1, data = train_data[, predictor_subset])
  x_test <- model.matrix(~ . -1, data = test_data[, predictor_subset])
  y_train <- as.matrix(train_data[, response_vars])
  y_test <- as.matrix(test_data[, response_vars])
  
  # Fit the model
  cv_model <- glmnet::cv.glmnet(
    x = x_train,
    y = y_train,
    family = "mgaussian",
    alpha = 0.5,  # Elastic Net
    nfolds = 5
  )
  
  # Optimal lambda
  lambda_opt <- cv_model$lambda.min
  
  # Make predictions
  predictions <- predict(cv_model, s = lambda_opt, newx = x_test)
  
  # Evaluate the model
  predictions_matrix <- predictions[, , 1]  # Use the first lambda slice
  
  metrics_list <- lapply(seq_len(ncol(y_test)), function(i) {
    actual <- y_test[, i]
    predicted <- predictions_matrix[, i]
    # Check lengths before calculations
    if(length(actual) != length(predicted)) {
      stop("Mismatch in lengths of actual and predicted values.")
    }
    rmse <- sqrt(mean((actual - predicted)^2))
    ss_total <- sum((actual - mean(actual))^2)
    ss_res <- sum((actual - predicted)^2)
    r_squared <- 1 - (ss_res / ss_total)
    data.frame(
      response = colnames(y_test)[i],
      rmse = rmse,
      r_squared = r_squared
    )
  })
  metrics_df <- do.call(rbind, metrics_list)
  metrics_df$predictors <- paste(predictor_subset, collapse = ", ")
  
  # Store actual and predicted values in a list
  results_list <- list(
    metrics = metrics_df,
    actual = y_test,
    predicted = predictions_matrix,
    predictors = predictor_subset
  )
  return(results_list)
}

# Fit and evaluate models
results <- lapply(predictor_combinations, fit_and_evaluate)

# Combine metrics into a data frame, include index
metrics_list <- lapply(seq_along(results), function(i) {
  metrics_df <- results[[i]]$metrics
  metrics_df$model_index <- i
  return(metrics_df)
})
results_df <- do.call(rbind, metrics_list)

results_df <- do.call(rbind, results)

# Convert 'response' and 'predictors' columns to factors for better plotting
results_df$response <- factor(results_df$response)
results_df$predictors <- factor(results_df$predictors)

# Add a column for the number of predictors used in each model
results_df$num_predictors <- sapply(strsplit(as.character(results_df$predictors), ",\\s*"), length)

results_df <- results_df |> arrange(rmse, r_squared) |> head(10)

ggplot(results_df, aes(x = reorder(predictors, -r_squared), y = r_squared, fill = response)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Model R-squared Values by Predictor Combination",
       x = "Predictor Combination",
       y = "R-squared") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1))

ggplot(results_df, aes(x = rmse, y = r_squared, color = response)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = as.factor(num_predictors)), size = 3) +
  labs(title = "RMSE vs. R-squared Across Models",
       x = "RMSE",
       y = "R-squared",
       shape = "Number of Predictors") +
  theme_minimal()


# For models with the best R-squared
best_models_r2 <- results_df |>
  group_by(response) |>
  filter(r_squared == max(r_squared)) |>
  ungroup()

# For models with the lowest RMSE
best_models_rmse <- results_df |>
  group_by(response) |>
  filter(rmse == min(rmse)) |>
  ungroup()
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)

# Function to plot predicted vs observed
plot_pred_vs_obs <- function(response_name, model_index, metric_df) {
  # Get actual and predicted values
  actual_values <- results[[model_index]]$actual[, response_name]
  predicted_values <- results[[model_index]]$predicted[, response_name]
  
  # Get RMSE and R-squared
  rmse_value <- metric_df |>
    filter(response == response_name, model_index == model_index) |>
    pull(rmse)
  
  r_squared_value <- metric_df |>
    filter(response == response_name, model_index == model_index) |>
    pull(r_squared)
  
  # Create data frame
  plot_data <- data.frame(
    actual = actual_values,
    predicted = predicted_values
  )
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = actual, y = predicted)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    labs(
      title = response_name,
      subtitle = paste("RMSE =", round(rmse_value, 4), ", R² =", round(r_squared_value, 4)),
      x = "Observed Values",
      y = "Predicted Values"
    ) +
    theme_few()
  
  return(p)
}

# Generate plots for models with best R-squared
plots_r2 <- list()

best_models_r2_list <- best_models_r2 |>
  group_by(response) |>
  summarise(model_index = first(model_index)) |>
  ungroup()

for(i in seq_len(nrow(best_models_r2_list))) {
  response_name <- best_models_r2_list$response[i]
  model_index <- best_models_r2_list$model_index[i]
  
  p <- plot_pred_vs_obs(response_name, model_index, best_models_r2)
  plots_r2[[response_name]] <- p
}

# Display plots
plot_grid(plotlist = plots_r2, ncol = 2)

# Generate plots for models with lowest RMSE
plots_rmse <- list()

best_models_rmse_list <- best_models_rmse |>
  group_by(response) |>
  summarise(model_index = first(model_index)) |>
  ungroup()

for(i in seq_len(nrow(best_models_rmse_list))) {
  response_name <- best_models_rmse_list$response[i]
  model_index <- best_models_rmse_list$model_index[i]
  
  p <- plot_pred_vs_obs(response_name, model_index, best_models_rmse)
  plots_rmse[[response_name]] <- p
}

# Display plots
plot_grid(plotlist = plots_rmse, ncol = 2)
```
